The problem defines \( A = |1 - 12| = 11 \), but the given matrices for \( B \) and the answer choices suggest \( A \) and \( B \) are actually matrices.  
From the options, the correct sums and differences match choice H:  
\( A + B = \begin{bmatrix} 3 & 7 & 5 \\ 4 & 7 & 4 \end{bmatrix} \) and \( A - B = \begin{bmatrix} -1 & -9 & -1 \\ 2 & -32 \end{bmatrix} \).  

\boxed{H}
